level_signals: 5-7+ years in Digital CS, Operations, Lifecycle Marketing, Growth, or Customer Success
Expected to build function from ground up and partner cross-functionally with Data Science and Engineering

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5-7+ years in Digital CS, Operations, Lifecycle Marketing, Growth, or Customer Success — with hands-on experience building scaled customer programs.
Systems-level thinking: you can translate a strategic objective into action by defining the requirements, scoping the intended impact , and building the measurement framework.
Fluency with AI-powered customer engagement — you've worked with chatbots, custom agents, or in-product AI experiences and can translate emerging capabilities into scalable program design.
Track record of cross-functional influence, particularly partnering with Data Science and Engineering teams to build infrastructure that didn't exist before.
Strong communication skills — you can present a program business case to leadership and write a tight requirements doc for a cross-functional partners.
Bias toward building new things, not just optimizing existing programs.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with segmentation and coverage model design, cost-to-serve analysis, or capacity planning.
Comfort designing global programs that scale across regions thoughtfully.
Familiarity with experimentation and A/B testing methodologies as applied to customer programs.

scope.surface: Digital CS segment — automated customer journeys, AI-powered engagement, attribution, and lifecycle programs at scale
primary_metrics_inferred: Customer outcomes across digital segment accounts
Program attribution and business impact measurement
Experimentation loop performance and program ROI
core_responsibilities: Build and optimize the global automated customer journey for the digital segment — segmentation logic, trigger architecture, and lifecycle programs.
Design and run experimentation loops for AI-powered engagement — guardrails, success metrics, and criteria for when to pivot a program.
Architect the attribution layer for Digital CS in partnership with Data Science to measure business impact.
Partner cross-functionally with Product, RevOps, Data Science, Enablement, and Marketing to align tooling, customer communications, and program design with scaled outcomes.
Own program reporting and iteration cadence — running the experimentation loop, surfacing insights, and making data-driven recommendations on where to invest and what to sunset.

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($170K-$210K total on-target earnings)
Small, high-impact team building function from the ground up — high ownership opportunity
Clear scope and cross-functional partners named explicitly
Encourages applicants whose past experience doesn't perfectly match every bullet
Equity mentioned as part of compensation
red_flags: Hybrid requirement is 3 days per week (Mon/Tue/Thu) in San Francisco — eliminates remote candidates
Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Compensation stated as total on-target earnings (base not broken out separately), making pure base comparison difficult
#LI-Onsite tag in JD but body describes hybrid anchor days — minor inconsistency in labeling

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years of experience requirement)
scope.team_size_estimate (inferred from 'small, high-impact team' language in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer base)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
compensation.base_range_usd (not stated separately; only total on-target earnings range provided)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: JD uses '#LI-Onsite' tag but body describes a hybrid model with 3 designated Anchor Days (Mon/Tue/Thu). Treated as hybrid here.
Compensation range ($170K-$210K) is described as 'total on target earnings (including base salary and on target incentive pay)' — base salary alone is not broken out.
Years of experience stated as '5-7+' which is an unusual combined range; treated as minimum 5, maximum 7 (with '+' indicating openness beyond 7).
